q: Electricity supplied to the house is a single-phase form of ___ current (AC).
The thick cable carrying electricity to the house contains two wires which are well ___ from each other.
One of the two wires in house wiring is the ___ wire (L).
The other wire in house wiring is the ___ wire (N).
The live wire (L) is a dangerous wire as it carries a ___ voltage.
The neutral wire (N) is usually at ___ volt.
Electricity is fed into the house through the main fuse, meter, main switch and then to the ___ box.
From the distribution box, there are normally three circuits: lighting, power (ring main), and ___ circuits.
The new colour code for wiring uses brown for live, blue for neutral, and striped ___ for earth.
The old colour code for wiring used red for live, black for neutral, and ___ for earth.
In the lighting circuit, the light bulbs are arranged in ___ across the mains line.
Since bulbs are connected in parallel, other bulbs are not affected when one bulb is switched ON or ___.
If bulbs are connected in series, switching OFF one bulb would switch ___ all the others.
The lighting circuit has only two wires: live and ___.
A lighting circuit must be connected sequentially as live wire, fuse, switch, load and then to ___ wire.
The power sockets in a house are connected by means of a ___ circuit.
In a ring main circuit, the live, neutral, and earth wires form a ___ of cable.
Power circuits are for appliances that consume ___ power than heating appliances.
Since current can flow to any particular socket by two ways in a ring main circuit, ___ wires can be used.
An earth wire (E) is added to the ring main circuit for ___ reasons.
Each main ring circuit uses a ___ A fuse.
___-pin sockets and three-pin plugs are used in the ring main circuit.
Heating circuits are for appliances such as electric cookers, ovens, hot plates, washing machines and ___.
Appliances connected in heating circuits use relatively ___ amounts of power.
Each fuse used in a heating circuit is rated at ___ A.
Circuits must be cared for not to be ___.
The fuse of each circuit must be connected in the ___ (L) lead.
A fuse connected in the neutral lead is ___.
A circuit breaker is an automatic switch which ___ (turns OFF) when the current rises above the specified value.
A circuit breaker can be reset by turning the switch ON or pressing a ___.
Earthing of an electrical appliance is the most important safety ___.
An earthing system connects specific parts of an electric power system with the ___.
In any electrical appliance, the ___ wire is always connected to the metal case.
If the live wire becomes loose and touches the metal body, the case of the iron would become ___ at high voltage.
With proper earthing, the current would pass directly into the earth through the earth wire, which has much smaller ___ than human being.
Lamps are connected in parallel in the lighting circuit so that the other bulbs are not affected when one bulb is switched ___ or OFF.
Fuses and switches are placed on the ___ wire to ensure the circuit is made safe by cutting off the high voltage supply.
The lighting circuit is the appropriate wiring circuit for ___ and fluorescent lamps.
The heating circuit is the appropriate wiring circuit for an electric ___.
The heating circuit is the appropriate wiring circuit for a water pump ___.
The heating circuit is the appropriate wiring circuit for an electric ___.
